location.official_address: Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar, Palaj, Gandhinagar, Gujarat 382055, India
location.nearest_airport: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport, Ahmedabad
location.nearest_metro: Dholakuva Circle Metro Station (approximately 11 km; no direct campus station)
location.technology_ecosystem: On-campus research park and incubation; access to GIFT City and the Ahmedabad-Gandhinagar research, finance and startup ecosystem
location.cost_of_living_band: Moderate; undergraduate life is predominantly residential and campus-based
location.data_origin: Official address and travel guidance; coordinates and some local distances are map-derived approximations

senior_junior_culture.summary: Official mentoring assigns small first-year groups to student guides; public evidence about day-to-day hierarchy is limited.
positive_themes: Public accounts mention help with projects and campus navigation
negative_themes: Insufficient independent evidence to generalize club hierarchy or batch interaction

academic_culture.summary: Official sources emphasize academic flexibility, research and project access. Student reports commonly describe approachable faculty, while experiences vary by professor and some describe a demanding workload.
negative_themes: Workload and disciplinary processes are described inconsistently

hostel_reality.summary: Official pages describe modern AC hostels, connectivity, security and common facilities. Recent public reports raise batch-specific room-space and maintenance concerns, so current occupancy should be verified.
negative_themes: Recent anecdotal space concerns in double rooms
Anecdotal complaints about water and repair response

club_and_campus_life.summary: Current official pages list 17 cultural and technical clubs and active councils. Independent reports disagree about the depth of club and tech culture.
positive_themes: Major institute festivals and strong sports infrastructure
negative_themes: Some students perceive limited club or competitive-tech intensity

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: Metis, SCALE and GRASP provide development, AI and competitive-programming platforms, while CDS supports placements and internships. Public student views on competitive coding and non-core preparation are mixed.
positive_themes: Dedicated development, ML and CP clubs
negative_themes: Some reports describe a smaller competitive-programming community and self-driven non-core preparation

locality_and_student_life.summary: The residential campus has strong road and metro-linked regional connectivity but is outside the main urban core, so last-mile travel is needed for frequent city activity.
positive_themes: Airport, rail and metro access with last-mile cabs/autos
negative_themes: Campus can feel isolated for students seeking daily city life

overall_satisfaction.summary: Evidence is polarized: many students value infrastructure, faculty access, research and flexibility, while others report dissatisfaction with placements, administration, maintenance or campus isolation. No numeric rating is justified.

parent_mode_summary.label: Perspective summary based on collected evidence
parent_mode_summary.summary: IIT Gandhinagar offers verified national-institute status, a modern residential campus, extensive financial aid and strong higher-study/research pathways. Parents should verify the 2026 hostel allocation, final fee demand, scholarship approval and final placement-cycle denominator before committing.
parent_mode_summary.cost_note: Calculated unchanged-rate full-tuition institutional/hostel/mess estimate is approximately INR 13.09 lakh; personal expenses and future revisions are excluded.
parent_mode_summary.placement_note: NIRF 2024-25 four-year UG median is 14 LPA, while annual-report placement percentages use registered-student denominators.
parent_mode_summary.travel_note: Airport and rail access is practical but the campus requires last-mile transport.
major_risks: Hostel occupancy may vary by batch

student_mode_summary.label: Perspective summary based on collected evidence
student_mode_summary.summary: IITGN may suit students who value research, academic flexibility, modern infrastructure and a smaller community with opportunities to lead. Students prioritising very large competitive-coding communities, frequent city life or only software placement outcomes should compare branch-level opportunities carefully.
student_mode_summary.branch_fit: Eight current BTech branches include AI and IC Design & Technology; historical dual-degree cutoffs should not be mistaken for current programmes.
student_mode_summary.campus_life: Officially broad clubs, festivals and sports facilities; independent views on intensity are mixed.
student_mode_summary.placement_roadmap: Build branch fundamentals and role-specific projects early; treat non-core preparation as student-driven unless company eligibility is officially confirmed.

decision_risks.risk: Historical cutoff interpreted as guaranteed 2026 admission
decision_risks.mitigation: Use 2023-2025 only as trend evidence and wait for official 2026 JoSAA rounds.

decision_risks.risk: Placement percentages compared across incompatible denominators
decision_risks.mitigation: Keep NIRF graduate cohorts, registered participants and job-offer counts separate.

decision_risks.risk: Assuming a donor scholarship is guaranteed
decision_risks.mitigation: Verify current notice, need/merit criteria and award decision.

decision_risks.risk: Expecting a specific first-year room-sharing pattern
Choosing a core branch solely for software placement expectations
decision_risks.mitigation: Check current company eligibility and build a role-specific roadmap.

decision_risks.risk: Expecting daily city access without last-mile travel
decision_risks.mitigation: Account for metro/cab/auto connections and campus shuttle schedules.
